# Allowlist for backdated migrations approved as history-only restorations.
# Each entry is a 14-digit timestamp (basename prefix of a migration file).
# Add a comment above each entry citing the PR + reason.
#
# A "backdated" migration is a local file whose timestamp is older than the
# most-recently-applied remote migration. The supabase-migrate.yml workflow's
# "Backdated-migration safety guard" step rejects any unallowlisted backdated
# migration before `db push --include-all` can apply it.
#
# Add entries here ONLY when:
#   1. The migration is a history-only restoration (the SQL was already
#      executed against the remote DB via a different path), AND
#   2. The body is idempotent (re-application is a no-op), AND
#   3. There is a corresponding PR documenting the reconciliation.

# PR #174 — history-only restoration of migration 092
# (trades_dedup_fill_full_unique). Index already live in prod; local file
# body byte-recovered from prod's schema_migrations.statements[] array so
# `supabase db push` sees matching version on both sides and skips the DDL.
20260430081243

# PR #176 — history-only restoration of enable_pg_cron. The pg_cron
# extension is already installed in prod; CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS
# is idempotent. File restored to reconcile local-vs-remote history.
20260513094906
